trajectory.description: Iteration 1 baseline exploration_score=0.1428 (success_rate 0.21). To improve, we need a learned planner. I'll implement a DQN-style agent using a CNN on the global belief map and a per-neighbor Q-value head. Architecture: small CNN (2 channels: belief map + robot heatmap) → feature map, then sample features at neighbor locations + node utility/distance → Q. Training: collect episodes with epsilon-greedy, sparse reward = 300/distance if success else 0, train with MSE on returns. I'll write model.py, utils.py, train.py, and update predict.py to load the model. Then train for 50 episodes as a quick test, save checkpoint, and submit evaluation to measure iteration time and see initial learning signal.

trajectory.description: Observation: iteration 2 train.py failed with grid_sample shape error due to double unsqueeze. Analysis: grid_sample expects 4D grid (B, H_out, W_out, 2), but code produced 5D. Also coordinate normalization must match feature map size. Decision: fix model.py to use unsqueeze(2) for (B, N, 1, 2) grid; rewrite utils.py to compute correct normalized coordinates for local window and feature map; adjust train.py accordingly. Then run a quick training test (few episodes) to verify pipeline, save checkpoint, and submit eval to measure iteration time and learning signal.

trajectory.description: Observation: DQN training in iter3 had zero rewards (dense reward missing), epsilon=1.0 for first 5 episodes, no learning. Analysis: sparse reward + few episodes + no target net caused no signal. Decision: implement shaped reward (explored_rate increment), experience replay with larger buffer, target network, longer training (500 eps), and evaluate immediately to measure progress. Estimated training time ~42 min, then submit eval.

Also incorporate node utility as additional feature for better learning signal. Decision: Rewrite model.py (add utility input), utils.py (include utility for neighbors), train.py (robust DQN with per-sample updates), predict.py (load model correctly). Run quick 100-episode training to validate pipeline, then submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: train.py failed with device mismatch error because preprocess_obs returns CPU tensors, but QNetwork is on GPU and input tensors aren't moved to device. Also grid_sample coordinates are normalized based on input image size (64x64) instead of feature map size (16x16), causing incorrect feature sampling and zero learning signal. Analysis: need to fix device placement (move tensors to device before forward) and correct coordinate normalization to feature map grid. Also replay buffer storing variable-length neighbors requires per-sample training (no batching yet). Decision: rewrite train.py with device fix, implement per-sample training loop, add checkpoint saving; fix utils.py coordinate normalization to feature map; minor fixes in predict.py for model loading. Run training for 100 episodes to verify pipeline, then submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: DQN training in iterations 3-11 produced exploration_score 0.0 (all failures), far below heuristic baseline 0.1428. The model policy fails to achieve any success. Analysis: The DQN architecture and training setup are not effective; limited time budget suggests focusing on heuristic planning improvements. Decision: Abandon DQN and develop a smarter frontier-based planner using global graph pathfinding (BFS) to high-utility nodes. This should increase success rate and reduce travel distance. I will update predict.py with a SmartPolicy that: (1) picks highest-utility neighbor if any > 0; (2) else selects the globally highest-utility node and navigates via BFS; (3) falls back to nearest frontier. I'll test this policy on a few training maps locally to verify it runs and improves success rate, then submit evaluation to measure against baseline 0.1428.

trajectory.description: Observation: Current predict.py (BFS+utility) gives 0% success in local test; robot explores only ~30% and stops. Analysis: The policy fails because when no node has utility>0 (likely due to undiscovered free cells), it falls back to unvisited nodes but may get stuck. Also visited_set prevents revisiting necessary nodes for pathing. Need a frontier-based planner that directly targets unknown/free boundaries (frontier cells) by pathing via global graph. Implement frontier detection on belief map, choose target frontier (random or farthest), navigate via BFS, allow revisiting with tabu short-term memory to break cycles. Also handle case when no frontiers remain (exploration complete) or no path to target. Submit eval to measure against baseline 0.5502.

Test: 0/15 success
trajectory.description: Observation: test_policy.py shows 0/15 success; explored rates are very low (~0.25) suggesting the robot stops early. Analysis: The HeuristicPlanner.select_action returns None when all node utilities are zero (common at episode start, as no frontiers scanned). This causes the episode to terminate immediately after the first few steps. The fix: when global_best_u == 0, fall back to a random neighbor (or the first neighbor) to continue exploring. Also fix reconstruct_path bug (goal_idx -> target_idx). This should allow the robot to keep moving and eventually discover frontiers, boosting success rate significantly. Decision: edit predict.py with this fallback, test locally to confirm success > 0.5, then submit eval to regain baseline ~0.55 and potentially exceed it.

trajectory.description: Observation: All recent local tests show 0% success with heuristic planner, explored_rate ~0.2-0.35, travel_distance ~800m. The robot moves a lot but explores poorly, indicating it fails to navigate towards frontiers effectively. The best score so far (0.5502) was from a simpler greedy-utility + BFS planner that worked in iteration 13, but the current implementations have bugs that cause early termination or ineffective movement. Analysis: The likely issue is that the planner gets stuck when all node utilities are zero (no visible frontiers) and the fallback mechanism fails because all immediate neighbours are in the tabu list, causing the episode to end prematurely. Also, path reconstruction or graph building may have bugs. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with a different strategy: frontier‑based exploration. Detect frontier cells on the belief map (FREE adjacent to UNKNOWN), choose a target frontier (farthest from robot or random), find the nearest graph node to that frontier, BFS to that node, and move one step along the path. This directly targets unexplored areas and should avoid the zero‑utility deadlock. Implement a robust fallback (if no path, move to random neighbour, no tabu) to ensure the robot never stops early. Test locally on 15 training maps to verify >0 success rate, then submit eval to get a new baseline. If success, use remaining iterations to refine path efficiency (e.g., utility‑weighted target selection).

trajectory.description: Observation: The heuristic planner crashes with IndexError in bfs_path because the graph is built once at episode start but the environment dynamically adds nodes as new free space is discovered. When select_action uses global_best_idx derived from current obs['nodes'] (which may have more nodes than the initial graph), bfs_path uses a seen list of length self.n_nodes (original) and goal_idx may exceed that, causing index out of range. Analysis: Rebuilding the graph each step will fix this and allow correct BFS to newly added nodes. The previous successful iteration (13) likely used a static graph but its environment version might not have dynamic node addition? Regardless, rebuilding each step is safe and correct. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with a UtilityGreedyPlanner that rebuilds graph on every select_action call (or at each step), robust fallbacks, and no early termination. Test locally to verify success > 0, then submit evaluation to regain baseline ~0.55 and potentially exceed it. Remaining iterations: 2, time: 3.15h — sufficient for one more eval submission.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter 29 predict.py crashed with AttributeError because 'nodes_coords' was not set before calling get_closest_node_idx in run_episode. The graph was only built inside select_action, which is called later. Analysis: The fix is to build the graph immediately after env.reset() and after each env.step, before any call to get_closest_node_idx. This ensures the graph is always up-to-date and indices are valid. The heuristic (greedy neighbor utility + BFS to global max) is the same approach that previously achieved 0.5502 exploration_score. With only 1 iteration remaining, we must produce a working predict.py that runs without crashes and submit evaluation. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with correct graph management, a robust run_episode loop, and full evaluation logic. Test import quickly, then submit eval using dummy checkpoint to get a final score. If it recovers the baseline, the final score will be at least 0.5502.
